title: Delicates wool wash
subtitle: A gentle castile soap and glycerin wash for hand-washing wool, cashmere, and silk
excerpt: Wool and silk require a pH-neutral wash with no agitation and no heat. This castile soap and glycerin blend is pH-close to neutral, conditions fibres as it cleans, and rinses out without leaving a residue. Use for hand-washing wool jumpers, cashmere, and silk blouses. Not suitable for machine washing.
sourceNotes: Wool fibre pH sensitivity: wool and silk protein fibres (keratin, fibroin) are damaged by alkaline detergents above pH 9, causing irreversible felting or loss of lustre. Castile soap diluted to 1 to 2% in water has a pH of approximately 7 to 8, which is safe for protein fibres: standard textile care reference. Glycerin as a humectant conditioner in textile washes: Corbman, Textiles: Fiber to Fabric (1983). Do not use distilled white vinegar as a rinse on silk; it can strip the lustre finish over repeated use.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. A 250 ml bottle provides at least 15 to 20 hand-washes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps indefinitely. Mix a bottle and use as needed.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keeps 12 months in a sealed bottle at room temperature. Discard if the mixture separates permanently or develops an unusual smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A substance that attracts and retains moisture. Glycerin in this wash acts as a humectant, keeping wool and cashmere fibres supple after washing.

content.text: Wool and cashmere felt when agitated in hot or highly alkaline water; the scale structure of wool fibres locks together and the garment shrinks irreversibly. A gentle wash requires cool water, no agitation, and a pH-neutral detergent. Liquid castile soap diluted in water is mildly alkaline and safe for wool at this concentration. Glycerin adds a
quality that keeps wool fibres soft. Use cool to lukewarm water only and handle washed garments with care; wool is significantly weaker when wet.
Combine the castile soap, glycerin, and water in the bottle. Swirl gently to mix. Do not shake vigorously; the soap will foam. The mixture should be a clear, slightly viscous liquid.
How to wash a wool or cashmere garment

Fill a basin with cool water. Add 1 to 2 teaspoons of the wash blend and stir gently to combine. Submerge the garment and press it gently into the water. Leave to soak for 5 minutes. Do not rub, wring, or agitate. Lift the garment, support its weight fully, and squeeze water out gently. Rinse by pressing the garment in clean cool water two or three times. Roll the garment in a clean dry towel and press to remove excess water. Lay flat on a towel to dry; do not hang, as wet wool stretches under its own weight.
Keep in a sealed bottle at room temperature. Keeps 12 months. Discard if the mixture changes colour or smells unusual.
